action: Proximity flip — reverse segment when pts.last is closer to prev_end_pt than pts.first
risk: pts stored end→start intentionally for some other consumer; reversal breaks that consumer
why: Animation requires pts in travel order. The end closer to the preceding track's exit is the departure end of this track. This is the only geometrically consistent interpretation.
promotion_path: Zero OS entries when all ring arcs are declared CCW in lines.json and id_lookup upgrade gives correct pts — flip becomes a no-op and can be removed

action: First-maneuver orientation — reverse maneuvers[0] if it reduces gap to maneuvers[1].first
risk: Departure direction wrong after reversal; pod would back out of platform rather than depart forward
why: First maneuver has no prev_end_pt seed so the proximity flip cannot fire. Reversing reduces the gap to the second maneuver, confirming departure direction.
promotion_path: Explicit departure direction stored in lines.json or trip plan removes the ambiguity

action: id_lookup upgrade — replace map.json pts with path.json/edge pts for every id_lookup entry where lookup has a better source
risk: path.json pts wrong for a track where map.json had correct pts; upgrade makes animation worse not better
why: path.json applies endpoint snaps and direction corrections that map.json does not. lookup already uses path.json as authority for the string-keyed path. id_lookup must use the same authority or build_maneuvers_from_ids proximity flip works against uncorrected coordinates.
promotion_path: When all endpoints are declared in lines.json successors and Step 6 closes all gaps, map.json and path.json are identical — upgrade becomes a no-op

action: Ring direction correction — reverse a ring arc (gw_c_N_M) when its last pt is closer to the preceding arc's last pt than its first pt is
risk: Arc intentionally stored end→start for some other reason; reversal breaks that consumer
why: The CCW ring traversal rule (Axiom 3 / all flows CCW) requires each arc's first pt to be the entry from the prior arc's exit. SketchUp ArcCurve extraction order is not guaranteed CCW. Reversing restores the declared traversal order.
promotion_path: When template designer runs Extract Template with explicit CCW annotation, Step 4 becomes a validation (confirm no reversal needed) rather than a fix

action: Ring junction snap — move gw_in_*/gw_out_* endpoints to nearest ring arc endpoint (within 500mm)
risk: Snapped to wrong ring arc endpoint; gw_in connects to wrong entry point on the ring
why: Step 2 (centerline correction +250mm) shifts ring arc endpoints inward. gw_in/gw_out endpoints stay at outside-rail position. The ~250mm gap is a coordinate-frame artifact, not a real gap. Snapping to the nearest ring endpoint restores the physical connection.
promotion_path: When extracted.json stores pts already in vehicle-path (centerline) frame, Step 2 correction becomes identity and this snap becomes a no-op

action: Bezier reconstruction for gw_in_*/gw_out_* — replace 2-pt chord with cubic Bezier using predecessor exit tangent and successor ring entry tangent
risk: Tangent vectors wrong (extracted backward or from wrong track); Bezier curves in wrong direction
why: gw_in/gw_out are curved transition tracks. A 2-pt chord gives a straight line that crosses guideway structure. Bezier from adjacent track tangents gives a physically plausible curve that animates smoothly.
promotion_path: When Extract Template stores gw_in/gw_out pts_mm directly (as ArcCurve extraction), reconstruction is not needed — use stored pts instead

action: Intra-station endpoint snap — move B.first to A.last for declared successor pairs where gap is 1–500mm
risk: A→B succession wrong in lines.json; snap moves wrong endpoint, breaking the real connection
why: SketchUp template geometry has sub-millimeter inconsistencies at track junctions. Declared successors are authored by the template designer — the successor declaration is authoritative. A gap of 1–500mm on a declared pair is a model construction artifact, not a real gap.
promotion_path: When template models are rebuilt with exact endpoint alignment, gap is 0 and snap becomes a no-op. Enforce via proof_lines check at Extract Template time.

action: Cross-track snap-candidates — apply endpoint snap for seg_*→gw_cp_in_* and similar cross-station-boundary pairs recorded in {model}.snap-candidates.json
risk: Snap-candidates entry is stale, wrong pair, or accumulated from a model configuration that no longer exists
why: Step 6 only covers declared intra-station successors. The seg→station boundary is not in lines.json successors. Animation measures the gap at runtime; snap-candidates.json accumulates confirmed pairs. Step 7 closes them in path.json so id_lookup upgrade gets correct pts.
promotion_path: Noelle promotes run_count≥2 candidates to lines.json successors. Once declared, Step 6 handles the snap and Step 7 becomes a no-op for that pair.
